responses: Hello!
Hi there!
Hey! How can I help you?
Artificial Intelligence is the simulation of human intelligence in machines.
An intelligent agent perceives its environment and takes actions to achieve goals.
A rational agent acts to achieve the best expected outcome based on available information.
Environments can be deterministic, stochastic, fully observable, or partially observable.
Search algorithms are used to explore problem spaces to find solutions.
BFS explores nodes level by level and guarantees shortest path in unweighted graphs.
DFS explores as deep as possible before backtracking.
A* is an informed search algorithm that uses heuristics to find optimal paths.
Knowledge representation is a method of encoding information so that machines can reason about it.
Prolog is a logic programming language used in AI for problem solving.
Probability measures the likelihood of an event occurring.
A random variable represents outcomes of a random process.
Mean is the average value and variance measures the spread of data.
Machine Learning is a subset of AI that enables systems to learn from data.
Supervised learning uses labeled data to train models.
Unsupervised learning finds patterns in unlabeled data.
Reinforcement learning trains agents using rewards and penalties.
Classification is a supervised learning task that assigns labels to data.
Clustering groups similar data points without labels.
Regression predicts continuous values.
Overfitting occurs when a model memorizes training data but fails on new data.
Underfitting occurs when a model cannot learn patterns in data.
Bias is error from assumptions, variance is error from sensitivity to data.
It refers to problems caused by high-dimensional data.
NLP enables machines to understand human language.
Transfer learning uses knowledge from pre-trained models.
Sentiment analysis determines whether text is positive, negative, or neutral.
Face detection identifies human faces in images.
You're welcome!
Happy to help!
Goodbye!
See you later!
